title: Get Code42 Employee Information
content:
Receives usernames from a Code42 Incydr alert and queries Active Directory for employee and supervisor information, if applicable.

Cortex XSOAR/XSIAM SOAR playbook from the Code42 pack (Code42, partner-supported).

Inputs — user: The username to query in Active Directory.; incidentid: The Palo Alto XSOAR incident ID that receives the employee information (optional)..

Outputs — Account.DisplayName: The display name of the user.; Account.Manager: The manager of the user.; Account.Username: The samAccountName of the user.; Account.Email: The email address of the user.; Account.department: The Department of the user..

Integrations/scripts used: Active Directory Query v2, IsIntegrationAvailable.

      name: Is Input an Email, Display Name, or Username?
      description: Retrieves detailed information about a user account. The user can be specified by name, email address, or as an Active Directory Distinguished Name (DN). If no filter is specified, all users are returned.
      type: condition
      iscommand: false
      brand: ""

      '#default#':
      - "4"
      Display Name:
      - "3"
      Email:
      - "2"
    separatecontext: false
    conditions:

      condition:
      - - operator: isExists
          left:
            value:
              complex:
                root: inputs.user
                transformers:
                - operator: RegexExtractAll
                  args:
                    error_if_no_match: {}
                    ignore_case: {}
                    multi_line: {}
                    period_matches_newline: {}
                    regex:
                      value:
                        simple: .*@.*
            iscontext: true
          right:
            value: {}
    - label: Display Name

      - - operator: isExists

                    error_if_no_match: {}
                    ignore_case: {}
                    multi_line: {}
                    period_matches_newline: {}
                    regex:
                      value:
                        simple: '[ ]+'
            iscontext: true
